headline: CATTLE CRASH THROUGH $250 FLOOR ON FEEDER BREAKDOWN
subheadline: Both complexes fold as boxed beef drops and Iran tensions ease energy premium.
lead: Live cattle broke $250.05, down 1.4%, with feeders leading the breakdown at $366.18, off 1.8%. The feeder weakness confirms what Monday's question asked: the support at $250 didn't hold, and the breakdown through key technical levels is real. Boxed beef dropped sharply and the Iran oil premium is evaporating as diplomatic channels open, removing the energy cost tailwind that propped up the complex all week.
the_takeaway: The cattle breakdown is real, feeders confirmed it first.
teaser: Both cattle complexes broke key support as energy costs crater and beef demand softens.

one_number.context: Feeders broke first and harder, down 1.8% versus 1.4% for live cattle. This is the tell: when feeders lead lower, it's not just profit-taking on the rally, it's real demand destruction working through the complex. The feeder premium to live cattle compressed as replacement demand dried up.

yesterdays_call.summary: Called that feeder strength confirmed the $252 floor with Brazilian import dynamics explaining the rally's momentum.
yesterdays_call.note: Today's feeder breakdown invalidated the thesis completely, confirming the complex couldn't hold technical support.

sections.title: Cattle Complex Breaks
sections.body: Live cattle closed $250.05, down 1.4%, with feeders leading the breakdown at $366.18, off 1.8%. The boxed beef cutout dropped sharply overnight, removing the demand premium that held the complex above $250 all week. Feeders broke harder, signaling replacement demand is drying up as input costs stay elevated despite crude's retreat. The technical picture turned decisively bearish: both contracts closed below their 10-day moving averages for the first time since the Iran crisis began.
sections.catalyst: Boxed beef cutout dropped sharply, removing demand premium
sections.bottom_line: Both complexes broke key support, feeders leading confirms real breakdown.
sections.vs_yesterday: Cattle: feeder weakness invalidated the support thesis completely.

sections.title: Grains Mark Time
sections.body: Corn eased to $4.66, down a penny and a quarter, while beans gained a quarter-cent to $11.93. The energy cost relief from crude's retreat isn't translating to grain demand yet, and old crop export sales hit a marketing year low at 5.2 million bushels for soybeans. Wheat managed a small gain to $6.13 as funds covered shorts, but the move lacked conviction. The grains are waiting for next week's China activity to reset the export pace.
sections.catalyst: Old crop soybean export sales hit marketing year low
sections.bottom_line: Energy relief not yet translating to grain demand, waiting on China reset.

sections.title: Energy Premium Evaporates
sections.body: WTI crude fell to $94.93, down 0.8%, as diplomatic channels with Iran accelerated behind the scenes despite ongoing military tensions around Hormuz. The first oil tanker reached South Korea through the strait since the conflict began, signaling shipping lanes are finding workarounds. Natural gas held flat at $2.78, but the energy cost tailwind that supported livestock input margins all week is fading fast.
sections.catalyst: First oil tanker reached South Korea through Hormuz since war began
sections.bottom_line: Diplomatic progress removing energy premium, input cost relief ahead.

outside_the_pit.title: DOJ Settles Agri Stats Meat Price-Fixing Case
outside_the_pit.body: Justice Department reached settlement with data company Agri Stats over antitrust allegations in meat pricing. The move aims to lower meat prices for consumers by ending alleged price coordination among processors.

outside_the_pit.title: USDA Restores Whole Milk in School Programs
outside_the_pit.body: Agriculture Department issued interim final rule implementing the Whole Milk for Healthy Kids Act. Schools can now offer whole milk in federal child nutrition programs, reversing years of low-fat requirements.

outside_the_pit.title: Canada Eyes Golden Opportunity in Middle East Crisis
outside_the_pit.body: International Energy Agency chief says Canada has a 'golden opportunity' to become major global oil player as Middle East war limits crude sources. The cost of missing this window will be substantial.

spread_to_watch.label: Live cattle / feeder ratio
spread_to_watch.commentary: The ratio compressed as feeders fell harder than live cattle, signaling replacement demand is drying up faster than finished cattle demand. When feeders lead the breakdown, it's telling you the problem is structural, not just profit-taking on the rally.

basis.headline: Livestock basis weakening as cash premiums fade
basis.body: Cash cattle premiums to futures narrowed across the Plains as packers backed away from aggressive bidding. The Iran energy premium that supported input cost margins is evaporating, giving processors less urgency to secure supplies at premium levels. Feeder basis particularly soft in the Southwest as drought concerns compete with weakening replacement demand.

weekly_thread.question: Will the cattle complex find support at $250 or continue the breakdown through key technical levels?
weekly_thread.status_text: The breakdown won. Cattle closed below $250, feeders led the way down, and the technical support that held all week finally gave way as energy costs retreated and boxed beef demand softened.

the_more_you_know.title: When Feeders Lead, Listen
the_more_you_know.body: Today's feeder breakdown to $366.18 wasn't just a bigger percentage move than live cattle's 1.4% drop. It was the tell. When feeders lead cattle lower, it signals replacement demand is drying up, meaning the problem isn't just profit-taking on recent gains but real demand destruction working through the complex. The feeder-to-live ratio compressed to 0.683 as replacement buyers backed away faster than finished cattle demand faded. This is how structural corrections begin: at the replacement level, not the cash level.

watch_list.desc: Weekly cattle slaughter data; under 650K head confirms demand softening.
USDA Crop Progress; corn above 45% planted removes weather premium.
Live cattle above $252 reclaims technical support, below $248 targets $245.
Weekly export sales; soy under 400K MT confirms China still backing away.

daily_quote.text: If we throw nature out the window, she comes back in the door with a pitchfork.

source_summary: CME Group, USDA, Brownfield Ag News, Oil Price, Farm Policy News
